summary: Patrick McKenzie announces that Bingo Card Creator has become profitable, exactly one month after launching the business. He had been worried after increasing his advertising budget but received enough orders to cross into profitability. He thanks the Joel on Software community for advice and credits teachers as his core customers, closing with a plug for his icebreaker bingo product.
key_points: Bingo Card Creator reached profitability one month after launch, despite concerns about increased ad spending
The advertising budget increase was modest — about 5 sales worth per month — showing how small the margins were at this stage
Community support from Joel on Software forums played a meaningful role in the business's early success
McKenzie naturally weaves product promotion into genuine gratitude toward his teacher customer base
money_quotes: I got some orders today and I'm now profitable and will continue to be for the forseeable future.
I also like that its one month to the day after opening my business.
key_insight: A tiny software business can reach profitability in just one month with modest advertising spend and a clearly defined niche customer base.
